puzzle.title: The Dispenser of Timeless Wisdom
puzzle.intro: A revered figure, known for their profound insights and sagacious advice, once imparted counsel that transcends generations. Their words, recorded in sacred scripture, guide individuals towards gratitude, patience, and righteous conduct.
clues: I was blessed with wisdom, not prophethood.
I advised my child on the greatest of sins: associating partners with Allah.
I taught about the weight of deeds, even a mustard seed's worth, and how Allah will bring it forth.
I emphasized gratitude to Allah and to one's parents, acknowledging the struggles of raising a child.
My counsel included establishing prayer, enjoining good, forbidding evil, and enduring patiently what befalls one.
A chapter in the Quran is named after me, containing my teachings and the lessons I shared.

outcome.label: Outcome
options: He was raised to the heavens

puzzle.arabic: وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا لُقْمَـٰنَ ٱلْحِكْمَةَ أَنِ ٱشْكُرْ لِلَّهِ ۚ وَمَن يَشْكُرْ فَإِنَّمَا يَشْكُرُ لِنَفْسِهِۦ ۖ وَمَن كَفَرَ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَنِىٌّ حَمِيدٌۭ
puzzle.verse: And We had certainly given Luqmān wisdom [and said], "Be grateful to Allāh." And whoever is grateful is grateful for [the benefit of] himself. And whoever denies [His favor] - then indeed, Allāh is Free of need and Praiseworthy. (31:12)
